About This Blog

A demo blog built with the Autumn web framework

This blog is built with Autumn — an opinionated, convention-over-configuration web framework for Rust. It assembles Axum, Maud, Diesel, htmx, and Tailwind CSS into a cohesive full-stack experience inspired by Spring Boot.

Tech Stack

  • •Axum — Fast, ergonomic HTTP routing and middleware
  • •Maud — Type-safe, compiled HTML templates
  • •Diesel — Safe, composable database queries
  • •htmx — Lightweight interactivity without JavaScript frameworks
  • •Tailwind CSS — Utility-first styling

Hybrid Rendering

This about page uses #[static_get] instead of #[get]. That means it can be pre-rendered at build time by autumn build and served as a static HTML file — zero compute per request. Dynamic routes like the post listing and admin panel continue to render on every request.

🍂 Fun fact: Autumn is the first Rust web framework with integrated hybrid rendering — static generation and server rendering in a single coherent stack.

Built with Autumn • Rust + Diesel + Maud + htmx + Tailwind
